figures.caption: Figure 1: The projection of γ onto the space spanned by α and β lies on a segment of a hyperbola (bold black line) whose endpoints correspond to γ = α and γ = β (when α′β ≥ 0) or to γ = α and γ = −β (when α′β < 0), shown here, and the opposite segment, not shown here. The orientation of the hyperbola and the endpoints depends on α′β.

Figure 2: RMSE, bias and standard deviation for simulated datasets. Each metric is plotted according to the deconfounding score coordinate parameter w; methods using base covariates are constant. In all plots, sT = 4 (low overlap) and sY = 5 (high SNR). See Section 5.1 for definitions of names of estimators.
Table 1: RMSEs on simulated datasets. Green: ϕγ̂(X) improves over X for the same base method; Red: it is worse. Best performance underlined. See Section 5.1 for definitions of names of estimators.

Table 2: RMSEs on semi-synthetic datasets. Green: ϕγ̂(X) improves over X for the same base method; Red: it is worse. Best performance underlined. See Section 5.1 for definitions of names of estimators.
